core_mechanism: coarsening / phase separation of a conserved two-phase field driven by interfacial-energy minimization but coupled to a long-range field-mediated (elastic strain / misfit) interaction, whose competition with the short-range interface energy selects domain morphology and can slow or arrest the coarsening
brief_reason: The abstract describes two coexisting phases coarsening under interfacial energy while a long-range elastic interaction from lattice misfit can accelerate, slow, or stop the process and reshape domains into plates, needles, or regular superlattices — a short-range vs. long-range interaction competition governing pattern coarsening.
